values:
GOL
(with Chris Holloway, Yung Scrilla & Ashton Travis)
[prod. Travis Scott]

OG Filename: GOL
Unknown year. Snippet leaked March 31, 2023.

OG Filename: just remake
Unknown year; has one unknown feature. Snippet leaked March 31, 2023.

New Girl
(with Chris Holloway)
[prod. Travis Scott]
Released at some point in late 2008 and referenced by some users on The Graduates' MySpace account, but was deleted and is presumably lost.

OG Filename: kanye free
Freestyle remix of the '808s & Heartbreaks' track, intended as a pitch for Kanye and GOOD Music to sign The Graduates; Travis reads out his phone number. 6 minutes long and no mumble. Leaked May 26, 2024.

OG Filename: kanye freestyle
Second version; follows a similar structure with similar lyrics. Version order is unclear. Leaked February 28, 2026.

Wanna Party
(with Chris Holloway)
[prod. Travis Scott]
Full song over the instrumental (track 2) from 'The Graduates EP'. Snippet leaked March 31, 2023.

Welcome to TX
(with Yung Scrilla)
[prod. Travis Scott]

OG Filename: welcome tx
Unknown year. Snippet leaked March 31, 2023.
Early version with no sample chop and no background vocals from Travis. Used in the trailer for the 'No Cigs No Guts' project.
Bape was originally just a song before it became a mixtape from April 2009. Based on that, 'Buddy Rich' was probably worked from April to December 2009. Unknown what differences this version has.
Released at some point, but was later deleted and likely lost. Referenced on KTT by user YoungxSho, who may've reuploaded it after it was deleted, but it wasn't archived.
Released at some point, but was later deleted and likely lost. Referenced on The Classmates' MySpace.

Old posts from April 2010 indicate that Travis and Chess were working on a tape called "Jam", with "McBride" being released as a single, though it seems that most songs from it ended up on the 'Cruis'n USA' deluxe. It's possible that the tape dropped and wasn't recovered.

No Cigs No Guts [Project]
(with OG Chess)
Mixtape that was in the works in 2011 and had a trailer made. Possibly evolved into 'Cruisin' USA'.
Unreleased song that was sampled in "Impossible", made by Allen in 2010 with some friends in New York. Included here since it doesn't really fit anywhere else.

An old tape mentioned by KTT user xxman as being a potential acquisition from Tony Loney. xxman later said (per Tony) that the tape contained songs that were mostly recycled songs into other projects done with Chess.

Pill$ and Coco Puff$ [Project]
(with OG Chess & Spuf Don)

Several tracks from 'Buddy Rich' released on MySpace prior to the project were tagged as being from 'Pill$ and Coco Puff$' (also the name of track 1), so it's possibly a project that evolved into 'Buddy Rich'.

An old tape mentioned by KTT user xxman as being a potential acquisition from Tony Loney. xxman later said (per Tony) that the tape contained songs that were mostly recycled songs into other projects done with Chess. Was supposed to be hosted by DJ Remy. A tracklist exists from October 21, 2010.

First Mention (Screenshot)

Tape Confirmation (Screenshots)

Twitter post (Screenshot)

⭐ 12 Disciples
[prod. FKi 1st & Travis Scott]
(AKA: Follow Me)

Leaked by Fitz on KTT on June 1, 2013, after he was sold a pack of early 'Owl Pharaoh' tracks by Pharaohxx. Presumably the song "Follow Me" from the August 2012 tracklist. According to Pharaohxx, Travis gave the song to Kendrick at SXSW 2013, but he never ended up using it. The instrumental appears to be a reworked version of the FKi-produced beat used for "Conclusion" with Machine Gun Kelly and Travis Porter. Technically released on the Grand Hustle compilation tape 'We Run This Vol. 10'.
Newer version with a "harder" beat intended for 'Owl Pharaoh', rumoured to be produced with Kanye. Mentioned on Tumblr by Travis, but the version on the project was unchanged.

Angels vs. Demons
[prod. Travis Scott]
(AKA: The City of Angels vs. Demons)

According to a tweet from Travis in September 2011, this song was to feature on a very early version of 'Owl Pharaoh'. Appears alongside "Cracks" on several tracklists leaked by L$, implying a two-part version.

Angels vs. Demons / Cracks
[prod. Travis Scott]
Two-part song that appears on several tracklists leaked by L$.

Animal (Remix)
(feat. T.I.)
[prod. TBHits, Kanye West & Travis Scott]

Reworked version with new drums from Kanye, announced on Twitter on January 5, 2013. A snippet was possibly played the same day (and again the following day) on Power 106, but all links appear to be dead, and KTT users who heard it all seemed to think that it sounded identical to the released version.

Initially made for an early version of 'Owl Pharaoh', likely in 2012, but Pharaohxx said it was being saved for an album that never materialised. An instrumental leaked on July 19, 2013, after being acquired from Tony Loney by KTT users Carlos and xxman. It was later used in 2016 by A$AP Twelvyy for "Lords Never Worry" with Nast and Rocky; no Travis vocals have ever surfaced.

Cover of Amy Winehouse's "Back to Black" that appears to be performed by Travis. Snippet originally leaked quietly on December 19, 2022, before being reposted on August 8, 2023. Said to be a reference for Beyoncé's version, but it's unclear why she would need a reference for a cover.

OG Filename: Bad Mood Rough Version 1
Early unfinished version disconnected from "Shit On You", with fewer verses and different lyrics. Leaked on July 19, 2013, after being acquired from Tony Loney by KTT users Carlos and xxman.

Bad Mood / Shit On You [Demo]
[prod. Emile Haynie & J Gramm]
Appears on the May 2013 tracklist. 5:04 long, 14 seconds shorter than release.

Bandz [Demo 1]
[prod. The Dope Boyz & MIKE DEAN]
(AKA: I Got Bandz)

Early version with a verse in place of Meek Mill played a couple of times in late 2012 and again at SXSW 2013. Leaked on July 19, 2013, after being acquired from Tony Loney by KTT users Carlos and xxman; Music Mafia later tried to auction it in 2018 as "I Got Bandz".

Bandz [Demo 2] 
(feat. Meek Mill) 
[prod. The Dope Boyz & MIKE DEAN]
Appears on the May 2013 tracklist. 5:24 long, 53 seconds longer than release. Possibly adds Meek's verse to the above demo, before it was decided that one of Travis's verses should be cut.

Different to the 'Birds'-era "Blame"; this is a new version of "Analogue" with Mr Hudson. Revealed by Pharaohxx on March 23, 2013; Travis confirmed its existence through a tweet three days later. Possibly intended for the scrapped iTunes deluxe version of 'Owl Pharaoh'.

Blocka La Flame [Demo 1]
[prod. Young Chop]
(AKA: Flocka)

Early version with some differing lyrics from the final release across both verses and no Popcaan. Also features the "Ill America" tag and pre-dates Mike Dean's contributions. Leaked October 8, 2019. Was originally Travis's song before Pusha T heard it and reworked it as his own for 'Wrath of Caine'. Titled "Flocka" at this stage, so it's likely the version found on the August 2012 tracklist.

Blocka La Flame [Demo 2]
[prod. Young Chop & MIKE DEAN]
(AKA: Blocka)
Teased in October 2012. Features Mike Dean applying his production.

Blocka La Flame [Demo 3]
[prod. Young Chop & MIKE DEAN]
(AKA: Blocka)

Near-complete; some of the vocal effects in the background are less noticeable, and it doesn't feature the background vocals found on the final version at around 2:52. Leaked on July 19, 2013, after being acquired from Tony Loney by KTT users Carlos and xxman.

🏆 Can't Look In My Eyes [V1]
(feat. Kid Cudi & Kanye West)
[prod. Anthony Kilhoffer, Kanye West, MIKE DEAN & Travis Scott]
(AKA: Fuck)

Song that appears on the August 2012 tracklist; production credits confirmed through tweets from Travis (Mike Dean was mentioned in a separate tweet). Posts from some KTT users and GOODMUSICINSIDER indicate that it is an early version of Kid Cudi's "Too Bad I Have to Destroy You Now" (previously/alternatively known as "Can't Look In My Eyes"), before Travis and Kanye were cut. Pharaoxx stated that "Fuck" was actually "Shit On You"; the evidence seems to suggest that he was wrong, but it's possible that both things are true, with "Fuck" developing into "Can't Look In My Eyes", and either the instrumental or Travis's vocals being used in some capacity on either "Bad Mood" (due to the heavy presence of the word "fuck") or "Shit On You".

KTT Posts (Screenshots)

August 2012 Tracklist (Screenshot)

Travis Tweet Transcriptions (Screenshots)

Kid Cudi - Can't Look In My Eyes [V2]
(feat. Kanye West & Travis Scott)
[prod. Kid Cudi & Dot da Genius]

Later version of "Fuck"; eventually became "Too Bad I Have To Destroy You Now". A snippet circulated on KTT in April 2013, but it appears to be lost. At a 2014 meet-and-greet, according to a KTT user, Travis expressed mild annoyance when "TBIHTDYN" was played over the speakers, stating: "Aye, turn this shit off. Me and 'Ye were supposed to be on this but [Cudi] took us off". According to another KTT user, Travis confirmed that Cudi had already reworked the beat by this point, likely bringing in the "Son of Flynn" sample.

Can't Look In My Eyes [V3]
(feat. Kanye West & Kid Cudi)
[prod. Kid Cudi & Dot da Genius]

Travis tweeted on March 26, 2013, that he wanted to drop "Can't Look In My Eyes" after Cudi cut it from his album, and he stated on Twitter that the original version was better after it released, which was probably in reference to "Fuck". This is likely the Kanye and Cudi song that Pharaohxx mentioned would appear on the iTunes deluxe version of "Owl Pharaoh", but the project never released and Cudi took the song back anyway. Leaked audio clips from an unknown time show what sounds like will.i.am calling the song "fresh" during a listening session of 'Owl Pharaoh'.

KTT Posts (Screenshots)

will.i.am Clip (Pillowcase)

Travis Tweet Transcriptions (Screenshots)

🏆 Chaz Interlude [Demo] 
(feat. Toro y Moi) 
[prod. Toro y Moi]
Appears on the May 2013 tracklist. 4:42 long, 3:02 longer than release.
An old song title referenced by L$ as being on some older versions of 'Owl Pharaoh'. Often written alongside "Angels vs. Demons", implying a two-part version.

Dance on the Moon [Demo 1]
(feat. Theophilus London) 
[prod. J Gramm]
(AKA: Dancing on the Moon)

Early version that doesn't feature Paul Wall. Travis's verses, which are the opposite way around, use an earlier take and have a few different lyrics. Possibly titled "Dancing on the Moon" at this point, as it appears as such on the May 2013 tracklist. Posted privately to a SoundCloud account on March 5, 2013, but wasn't made publicly available until August 18, 2016.

Dance on the Moon [Demo 2]
(feat. Theophilus London & Paul Wall) 
[prod. J Gramm]
Appears on the May 2013 tracklist. 4:04 long, 1:10 shorter than release. Possibly missing a Travis verse.

In an interview with TheDrop.fm, Travis stated that the "real "Drive"" had him freestyling over an instrumental produced by Lil' Flip "for like the first minute". Presumably an early concept that evolved into "Drive"; the instrumental was likely "I Can Do Dat", which was sampled in the released version's intro.

Drive [Demo 2]
(feat. James Fauntleroy) 
[prod. Kanye West, No I.D., Travis Scott & Ann Marie Calhoun]
Early version that seems to feature some minor production differences.

Drive [Demo 3]
(feat. James Fauntleroy) 
[prod. Kanye West, No I.D., Travis Scott & Ann Marie Calhoun]
Appears on the May 2013 tracklist. 5:17, 3 seconds longer than release.

Leaked by Fitz on KTT on June 1, 2013 after he was sold a pack of early 'Owl Pharaoh' tracks by Pharaohxx; Travis repeatedly teased its release around August 2012 and beyond. Can be seen on the August 2012 tracklist, and Travis teased a music video through now-deleted Facebook and Tumblr posts.
A song referenced by Pharaohxx as being intended for 'Owl Pharaoh' at some stage. Pharaohxx listed it as a song where Travis displays his rapping ability, alongside "MIA", "Bandz", and "Bad Mood".

OG Filename [?]: Fall ( prod Travis Scott)
Song seen in a screenshot posted to Will & Rich's Tumblr. Unsure when it was posted, but KTT users discovered it in April 2013. Possibly intended for 'Owl Pharaoh' at some point judging by the cover art.

According to L$ and a tweet from Travis in September 2011, this song was to feature on a very early version of 'Owl Pharaoh'. An instrumental supposedly produced by Travis leaked on July 19, 2013, after being acquired from Tony Loney by KTT users Carlos and xxman. L$ stated that Travis called this song his favourite.

September 2011 Tracklist (Screenshot)

L$ Favourite Song Comment (Screenshot)

Instrumental supposedly produced by Travis that leaked on July 19, 2013, after being acquired from Tony Loney by KTT users Carlos and xxman. Strangely, this song does not feature on any of the known early tracklists for 'Owl Pharaoh', so it may have been renamed or quickly scrapped.

Supposedly produced by Travis, the instrumental was leaked by Fitz on KTT June 1, 2013, after he was sold a pack of early 'Owl Pharaoh' tracks by Pharaohxx. No vocals have surfaced, if they were ever recorded.

Posted on KTT by user YoungxSho on the same day that the song dropped; contains a new, real intro, and some extra effects and reverb not found on the released version. YoungxSho noted in several KTT posts that he was a friend of Travis and had leaked a couple other things in the past, so it's possibly just a leak that he wanted some clout for; he claimed to have made it, but he would've needed access to the stems for it so it's likely legitimate. Travis himself tweeted that he was going to re-release the song after being unhappy with the mix that made the XXL Freshman tape, but he never did - this could be the version that he wanted to drop.

OG Filename: XXL TRAVIS - ROUGHMIX NAB
Uncut version; has the same intro as the remastered version, and the instrumental runs for two more minutes after the vocals end, developing into a drop towards the end of the track. Possibly the version that Travis intended to drop. Leaked April 6, 2024.
Unfinished scrap that eventually developed into 'Old English'. Leaked by Fitz on KTT on June 1, 2013, after he was sold a pack of early 'Owl Pharaoh' tracks by Pharaohxx.

🏆 Gold 88 [V2] 
(feat. Pusha T) 
[prod. THC & Travis Scott]
Later version featuring Pusha T that appeared on the August 2012 tracklist. Apparently owned by Pharaohxx, but he purposefully left it out of the pack he sold, as he anticipated it would leak.

Hell of a Night [Demo 1]
[prod. DJ Dahi & Rakhi]
In a now-deleted tweet (that is probably completely lost), Travis stated that he recorded a longer version of the first part of the song, but he wasn't confident that it was good.

Hell of a Night [Demo 2]
[prod. DJ Dahi, Rahki, Rey Reel & Travis Scott]
Appears on the May 2013 tracklist. 3:16 long, 2 seconds longer than release.

According to info from L$ and a tweet from Travis in September 2011, this song was to feature on a very early version of 'Owl Pharaoh'. An instrumental supposedly produced by Travis leaked on July 19, 2013, after being acquired from Tony Loney by KTT users Carlos and xxman. L$ stated that the song was supposed to sample "Maniac" by Kid Cudi; it's not known if a version with the sample exists.

Remake of an old OG Chess song created in November 2011 that featured A$AP Ferg, produced by Travis; Travis was accused of stealing the instrumental by Shane Morris (his old manager) and a KTT member that knew Travis personally, with the possible intention of giving it to Kanye. Said by Travis to be for 'Owl Pharaoh' during his 2013 SXSW set and XXL Freshman interview, and Pharaohxx later implied that it would be on the iTunes deluxe edition, but it never saw a release, most likely due to the possible legal issues surrounding it. The instrumental leaked on July 19, 2013, after being acquired from Tony Loney by KTT users Carlos and xxman; the actual track leaked in a four-pack on May 16, 2016, which pissed Travis off.

OG Chess Tweet (Screenshot)

Song Origin Breakdown (Screenshot)

Song (KrakenFiles)

OG Chess x A$AP Ferg Version (Pillowcase)
Leaked by OG Drewbie, an ex-friend of Travis, on March 25, 2013, after KTT users tricked him into thinking they were Travis and annoyed him enough to leak it.

According to info from L$ and a tweet from Travis in September 2011, this song was to feature some early versions of 'Owl Pharaoh' (likely the song "Love"). It leaked in 2012 on YouTube seemingly out of the blue, labelled as being track 6 with the original 'Owl Pharaoh' cover art embedded. Samples "I Got a Woman" by Ray Charles. Teased in a (now-deleted) video posted to The Classmates' Facebook page in 2011.

Appears on the August 2012 tracklist; the instrumental was leaked by Fitz on KTT June 1, 2013, after he was sold a pack of early 'Owl Pharaoh' tracks by Pharaohxx. No vocals have surfaced, but they likely exist.

OG Filename: medow creek 04.06.2013
Appears on the May 2013 tracklist; dated April 6, 2013. 1:36 long, 12 seconds shorter than release.
Early version that has a slightly different beat and a different intro. Posted privately to a SoundCloud account on May 13, 2013, but wasn't made publicly available until August 18, 2016.
Version referred to as v2.5 that appears on the May 2013 tracklist. 4:26; 4 seconds shorter than release.

⭐ Momma Be Trippin
[prod. Amplive & Travis Scott]
(AKA: Last Night)

Throwaway from early versions of 'Owl Pharaoh'; likely to be the song "Last Night" from the August 2012 tracklist. OG Drewbie, an ex-friend of Travis, threatened to leak it on March 25, 2013; it leaked independently of him the same night. Technically released on the Grand Hustle compilation tape 'We Run This Vol. 10'.

Leak Explanation (Screenshot)

Leak Threat Tweet (Screenshot)

August 2012 Tracklist (Screenshot)

Naked [Demo 1]
(feat. Rick Rubin) 
[prod. J Gramm, Travis Scott & Rick Rubin]

Alternate version that is much longer and fleshed out, but unfinished. Rick Rubin speaks during the intro, and can be heard throughout the track. Posted privately to a SoundCloud account on March 5, 2013, but wasn't made publicly available until August 18, 2016.

Naked [Demo 2]
(add. Rick Rubin) 
[prod. J Gramm, Travis Scott, Ann Marie Calhoun & Rick Rubin]

Version with violin at the end from Ann Marie Calhoun, as well a snare created from her drumming against the violin. Likely wasn't recorded properly as it was merely an idea from Ann. Posted privately to a SoundCloud account on May 10, 2013, but wasn't made publicly available until August 18, 2016.

Naked [Demo 3]
(feat. Justin Vernon) 
[prod. J Gramm, Travis Scott & Rick Rubin]

Later version with a rough version of the final first verse, and extra autotune on Justin Vernon's vocals. A snippet recorded in the studio was leaked by KTT user Hdz on July 14, 2013, but was partially lost for over a decade; Justin's part was widely circulated as part of an edit, but Travis's vocals were always missing.

Naked [Demo 4 / Uncut Version]
(feat. Justin Vernon; add. Rick Rubin) 
[prod. J Gramm, Travis Scott & Rick Rubin]
Version that appears on the May 2013 tracklist; Pharaohxx stated that the version put on the project was a teaser, since Rick Rubin wanted to develop the track further.

⭐ Naked [Rick Rubin Rework] 
(feat. Justin Vernon) 
[prod. J Gramm, Travis Scott & Rick Rubin]

OG Filename: Travis_Scott_Bon_Iver_Naked_Final_Verses_2 2
Reworked version intended for the iTunes deluxe version of 'Owl Pharaoh' that never came out, and later intended for an album set to release in 2014 that was ultimately scrapped. Features an additional verse from Travis and vocals from Justin Vernon of Bon Iver. Leaked May 10, 2023.

Supposedly from 2011, per Pharaohxx, though KTT user Lord Elm, who owned the song prior to it leaking, claimed that it was from summer 2012. KTT users claimed that Travis said in a now-deleted tweet that it was intended as a reference for another artist, presumably Kanye, but finding a direct source for this has been difficult; he did, however, definitely say "fuck that night song" after it leaked. Uses the same instrumental sample as Trouble's "Molly World", featuring Future; most likely coincidence and not a sample. This unmastered version leaked November 5, 2021.
Better mixed version. Accurate version numbers for 'V2'-'V4' are unclear. Leaked March 13, 2013.
Slightly different mix; otherwise identical. Leaked by Fitz on KTT on June 1, 2013, after he was sold a pack of early 'Owl Pharaoh' tracks by Pharaohxx.

OG Filename: Night
Another slightly different mix; otherwise identical. Leaked October 19, 2022.

Old English [Mike Dean Rework] 
(feat. King Chip & Ashton Travis) 
[prod. THC, Travis Scott & MIKE DEAN]

Reworked version with production from Mike Dean intended for 'Owl Pharaoh' in early 2013, but was left off in the end; Travis's verse is also the first verse now. Performed with Mike at some point, possibly in mid-February 2013, but all links to it appear to be dead. The real thing hasn't leaked, but a quite accurate fan-made recreation was posted to KTT on February 27, 2013. The only remaining snippets online were posted after the recreation, so it's unknown what audio the recreation was based on.
Previewed by Travis on Vine on April 19, 2013.
Instrumental track; served as the intro to early versions of 'Owl Pharaoh'. Leaked by Fitz on KTT on June 1, 2013, after he was sold a pack of early 'Owl Pharaoh' tracks by Pharaohxx.
Leaked on July 19, 2013, after being acquired from Tony Loney by KTT users Carlos and xxman. Open-verse.
Leaked by Fitz on KTT on June 1, 2013, after he was sold a pack of early 'Owl Pharaoh' tracks by Pharaohxx. Features Travis trying to create a verse, as well as the chorus from 'V2'.
Appears on some early tracklists provided by L$ that were emailed to him by Travis. Confirmed to be a different song from "Owl Pharaoh Intro", as Pharaohxx offered it to KTT user xxman separately.

Leaked by Fitz on KTT on June 1, 2013, after he was sold a pack of early 'Owl Pharaoh' tracks by Pharaohxx. "Phoenix 9.0" can be seen on the August 2012 tracklist, though it's unconfirmed if this is the same version. Features vocals from an unknown female artist.

Pussy [Demo]
(feat. Chuck Inglish) 
[prod. Mike WiLL Made-It]
(AKA: Changed My Life, Thank You For Changing My Life)

Early version without Gunplay or Fredo Santana. Features a verse from Chuck Inglish that was cut, some different lines from Travis, and an open verse. Also lacks the "Been Trill" tags. Titled "Thank You For Changing My Life" on an early 'Owl Pharaoh' tracklist, per Pharaohxx (assuming it's the same version). Leaked as "Changed My Life" on July 19, 2013, after being acquired from Tony Loney by KTT users Carlos and xxman.

Quintana [Demo]
[prod. Sak Pase, Travis Scott & Anthony Kilhoffer]

Early solo version with an unheard verse, semingly from when it was first being recorded. Posted privately to a SoundCloud account on March 5, 2013, but wasn't made publicly available until August 18, 2016.

Quintana (Remix) [Demo 1]
(feat. Quavo & Takeoff) 
[prod. Sak Pase, Travis Scott & Anthony Kilhoffer]

OG Filename: 01 QUINTANA MIGOS REMIX
Early version featuring the same intro as the original song, minus Travis's vocals, and has an open verse, presumably for Offset, who was arrested before he could record for it. Leaked December 19, 2022.

Quintana (Remix) [Demo 2]
(feat. Quavo & Takeoff) 
[prod. Sak Pase, Travis Scott & Anthony Kilhoffer]

OG Filename: Quintana Remix 2.0
Later version that is nearly identical to the final release, but doesn't have the new intro from the Migos yet. Leaked December 19, 2022.

⭐ Sin City [Demo 1] 
(feat. Diana Gordon) 
[prod. TBHits & Travis Scott]

Very early version of "Sin City" with Diana Gordon (f.k.a. Wynter Gordon) on the chorus and an extra verse from Travis, originally intended for an earlier version of 'Owl Pharaoh' before Kanye took it for 'Cruel Summer' (it doesn't appear on any known tracklists, but now-deleted tweets from Travis essentially confirmed that it was intended for it at some stage). A low-quality version was leaked on KTT on June 1, 2013, by someone who worked with Tony Loney (and presumably Travis). Pharaohxx claimed to have it in HQ, but he purposefully left it out of the pack of demos that he sold to KTT user Fitz, as he anticipated that it would leak.

Very early song from 2011 that appears on a few tracklists. An instrumental that's essentially a loop of "Biscuit" by Portishead leaked on July 19, 2013, after being acquired from Tony Loney by KTT users Carlos and xxman. Part of this instrumental was previously used in an ILLROOTS promotion and was known as "iLLworld" on KTT; the audio is gone, but the original article from January 25, 2012, can still be accessed, with Travis being credited for the music.

⭐ Time Machine
[prod. MIKE DEAN & Travis Scott]
Leaked by Fitz on KTT on June 1, 2013, after he was sold a pack of early 'Owl Pharaoh' tracks by Pharaohxx; can be seen on the August 2012 tracklist.

✨ Trap In Mexico [V1]
(with Trae Tha Truth)

Likely made sometime in early - mid-2012 and, according to Pharaohxx, serves as an early concept for "Quintana". KTT user Lord Elm leaked snippets of the song in early 2013, but they are likely lost. Likely the same version that was 'released' by DJ S.R. on January 27, 2016.

Supposedly produced by Travis, the instrumental was leaked by Fitz on KTT on June 1, 2013, after he was sold a pack of early 'Owl Pharaoh' tracks by Pharaohxx. No vocals have surfaced, if they were ever recorded.
An old song title that appears on a few early tracklists for 'Owl Pharaoh'.

Untitled
(feat. King Chip) 
[prod. Travis Scott]
(AKA: Travis x Chip)

Leaked by Fitz on KTT on June 1, 2013, after he was sold a pack of early 'Owl Pharaoh' tracks by Pharaohxx; likely the untitled collaboration seen on the August 2012 tracklist. "Around Me" was a name given to it by KTT members after it leaked; the file was titled "Travis x Chip", though this is likely not the OG filename.

OG Filename: TRAVIS KIRKO MD 1 add to 2 track
Untitled collaboration likely recorded after the two met for the XXL Freshman cover; possibly the song that was rumoured on KTT to be dropping in July 2013 (unclear where this rumour originated). Leaked July 6, 2021. The file was leaked with a fake date at the end, which was added by a scammer.

Upper Echelon [Demo 1]
[prod. J Gramm, Anthony Kilhoffer, Travis Scott & MIKE DEAN]
Early version with an unfinished second verse and no features. Leaked on July 19, 2013, after being acquired from Tony Loney by KTT users Carlos and xxman.

Upper Echelon [Demo 2 / TV Track]
(add. T.I.)
[prod. J Gramm, Anthony Kilhoffer, Travis Scott & MIKE DEAN]

OG Filename: Upper_Echelon_EDIT_CLEAN_MTV
Version with a completed second verse and additional production from Mike Dean; also features minor additional vocals from T.I. Performed as part of Travis's main SXSW 2013 set, as well as during the MTV Woodies Showcase at the same event. Clearly intended as a performance version, but it remains listed as a demo due to the new vocals. Leaked December 25, 2022. Censored until the new verse starts; the last hook is uncensored.

Upper Echelon [Demo 3]
(feat. T.I. & 2 Chainz)
[prod. J Gramm, Travis Scott, Anthony Kilhoffer, MIKE DEAN & Kanye West]
Appears on the May 2013 tracklist. 4:06 long, 3 seconds longer than release.

Remix that may have been intended for the iTunes deluxe version of 'Owl Pharaoh'. Teased by Travis on April 29, 2013, via Vine; another (deleted) Vine posted by Tony Loney showed that TNGHT were in the studio at the time, likely meaning they produced it. A Kanye West feature was rumoured because of a tweet from Travis, but the tweet happened before the remix was previewed; Drake was rumoured as Travis was at his house the night before the snippet was posted, but this was pure speculation, and Travis later said (possibly sometime in 2014) that they hadn't worked together.
Teased on Travis's Vine on May 13, 2013. Appears to lack A$AP Ferg; Travis's part was likely finished.
Appears on the May 2013 tracklist. 4:11 long, 18 seconds shorter than release.

Early version that uses a different vocal take with some different lyrics, and lacks the pitched "ILLAMERICA" chant, with Travis singing it normally in some places instead. Originally titled "ILL". Leaked July 6, 2021.

OG Filename: TRAVIS ILL SONG
Near-complete version with some missing production elements. Leaked sometime in 2021-2022 (April 2022 is the furthest I can trace it, but it was definitely earlier).

On September 21, 2011, Chase B tweeted that Ashton Travis and Travis Scott made a song together that sounded like "a serious hit". Ashton also said on his Tumblr in mid-2012 that they had a few songs together.

Chase B Tweet (Screenshot)

Ashton Travis Tumblr Post (Screenshot)

[Unknown]
[prod. Hit-Boy, Hey DJ Camper & MIKE DEAN]
Hit-Boy and Travis both mentioned in interviews that the instrumental for JAY-Z's "Somewhereinamerica" was originally intended for Travis's album, before he gave it to Jay.

will.i.am was present during several 'Owl Pharaoh' sessions, and a clip of Rick Rubin talking suggests that he produced a reference that was given to Will. This version was played for Rick and several other people during an 'Owl Pharaoh' listening session; Travis implies that this is an earlier version with hi-hats that were cut.
Version with different/no hi-hats that was presumably sent to will.i.am.

An early cover art, featuring thirteen owls in the shape of a circumflex. This art was posted to Travis's Tumblr in 2012, and was embedded in the "16 Chapels" file posted to his website. This file also had the album metadata as 'Owl Pharaoh', so it's safe to assume that it was the cover art for a time.

Picture posted to Instagram shortly before the release of the free version of the project. While no primary source is available, when KTT user Carlos 'revealed' this information, multiple other KTT users stated that another source had already told them. Should probably be in the unconfirmed section for now.

While it wasn't officially announced until later, Travis was openly working on a studio album alongside 'Owl Pharaoh'; Pharaohxx said that was originally meant to release in September 2013. Travis stated that the instrumental for JAY-Z's "Somewhereinamerica" was intended for the project, and he also said that he wanted to put "Can't Look In My Eyes" on it after Cudi initially scrapped it. According to Pharaohxx, "Armageddon" was also being saved for the album. The project became Travis's main focus after the iTunes deluxe edition of 'Owl Pharaoh' was scrapped (see 'V2').

Stadium Red

Song of unconfirmed nature recorded at Stadium Red in New York, supposedly labelled as being part of 'Owl Pharaoh' at some stage. KTT user William made a post on February 24, 2015, stating that they had found the session while at the studio, claiming that it was a mumble reference with a "hard" beat. William then bounced it, and the file he posted contained adlibs from Travis, over the instrumental for what became "No Way Out" by Chinx. It now appears to be lost so it's hard to confirm its legitimacy; William's original comments about it being a mumble demo conflicts with what he leaked, but he may have only bounced the adlibs on purpose. Insider skr8up claimed in the thread that William would be facing legal action as a result, so that might make it more likely to be real. [NOTE: if you know anything about this, please contact an editor.]

Pharaohxx also supposedly said that it was real, but this is according to Carlos who was quoting an email from him. During an interview on HOT97 on July 16, 2013, Travis officially announced that he was working on an album, set to be released in 2014. At the time of the announcement, Travis said that he had recently been working with Crada, so any songs made with him are likely in contention. Pharaohxx also said that the Rick Rubin rework of "Naked" would make the album. On October 31, 2013, a Rolling Stone article said that Travis was considering 'Fuego' as a title, but 'Rodeo' was eventually settled on (in March 2014 at the latest). The project continued to evolve until eventually all songs from this time had been cut.

New Idea 1
[prod. Metro Boomin, Allen Ritter & Travis Scott]

OG Filename*: METRO_ALLEN_TRAVIS NEW IDEA 1
Throwaway idea from the same session as "Wine and Hennessy", produced by Metro Boomin and Allen Ritter. Referred to as "Marietta" in the community but never actually given an official title, with the session files being titled "METRO_ALLEN_TRAVIS NEW IDEA 1". The full bounce is an hour long, and contains three separate freestyles from Travis; time inbetween these freestyles is spent working on the instrumental. The third freestyle from the session leaked June 28, 2019. The full bounce leaked May 26, 2024. Two files are linked; one file is muted in places and is technically the correct bounce, while the other is unmuted, revealing extra freestyle vocals. The unmuted version features Travis and Metro talking about Justin Bieber recording for "Drunk", Metro talking about working with 21 Savage, and Travis criticising the sequencing of Meek Mill's 'Dreams Worth More Than Money', among many other things.

We are aware of the existence of the YouTube session edit of "Marietta", and it was linked on the Tracker before the entire session recording leaked. However, the edit will not be linked anymore as we now have the full session recording.

It's likely that Juicy is still on this version, with Travis wanting Krule to do a hook that was scrapped in favour of Pimp C. for whatever reason, but this is pure speculation. Wine and Hennessy
[prod. Metro Boomin & Allen Ritter]

OG Filename*: WINE AND HENNESY IDEA
Features Travis laying down reference vocals for about ten minutes over an evolving beat produced by Metro Boomin and Allen Ritter. Privately sold by Music Mafia and then previewed via an edited snippet under the name "All The Time"; it was labelled as featuring The Weeknd, but this was an intentional false claim to catch scammers. Sometimes shortened to "Hennessy" before the title was confirmed as "WINE AND HENNESY IDEA" (with the misspelling). The song wasn't bounced, and Music Mafia didn't even attempt it, so it leaked without the vocal effects that it was intended to have on February 6, 2018. An inaccurate recreation surfaced in November 2020. A completely accurate bounce leaked September 7, 2022.

Appears on The Weeknd's playlist in a Beats 1 collaborative commercial debuted at the MTV VMAs on August 30, 2015. Realistically, this makes no sense; the Kanye tracker previously speculated that this was the last version before it was given to Travis, but he clearly had the song long before then. Even if we assume it was made much earlier, it's still much longer than any other version, which only adds to the confusion; Kanye also didn't finish his vocals until very late in the 'Rodeo' sessions, so it probably can't be from earlier. A censored snippet can be heard in the commercial, but it essentially sounds like the released version.

✨ Kanye West - Can U Be [Demo 1]
(feat. Travis Scott)
[prod. Havoc]
(AKA: Pressure)

OG Filename*: trav uzi pressure
Kanye throwaway that was worked on in 2014-15, originally starting out as "Get Off Me"; this final known version features Travis and was made during 'SWISH' sessions (see the Kanye tracker for information on other versions). Another version had a snippet leak through Music Mafia in mid-2017. This version was teased on Instagram Live by Che Pope, on October 22, 2020. The intro samples the 2012 film 'Silver Linings Playbook', while the synths are sampled from "Haunted", from the 'Maniac' soundtrack. Uzi was present in the studio, but didn't record for this song. Leaked May 26, 2024. The first released version was identical to this leak.

Kanye West - Champions [Demo 2]
(feat. Travis Scott)
[prod. Low Pros & Menace]

Solo version previewed by Menace on November 15, 2017; snippets are lost. Exact year is unclear but it was likely Kanye's song at this point. A fake version was leaked January 30, 2018, by a suspected Logan Smitherman (RIP) alt. He said via DM that it was produced by Mike Dean, Menace, Diplo, and Charlie Heat; he also stated that it started out as a Travis x Diplo song, which is definitely false. Seems to be real to some degree, as it has a realistic beat similar to Demo 1 and has the full hook vocals without sounding like it was a DIY acapella, but the quality and cuts make it sound fake, and he was known to have the stems/potentially the project files. He also claimed to have "cleaned it up", so, even if this is real, it's still edited.

Seemingly untitled judging by the filename, but the original seller was adamant that it was titled "Night".

Recorded in October 2016; previewed by Travis on Beats 1 on April 3, 2017, and later briefly at a show. Likely missing Travis's bridge, or at least the conversation in the background; also features an early mix and is thought to have some different adlibs/lyrics. A snippet recorded by Quavo exists, featuring Travis recording reference vocals, but another snippet from the same session show that these vocals were finished immediately after and weren't used on an earlier version.

Version of the album that leaked December 21, 2017, just a few hours before it dropped officially. Everything is much louder than the final version; Mike Dean rushed to remaster it quickly after the leak.
Solo Quavo version, lacks Travis Scott's verse. Identical to 'V2' besides that. Leaked January 11, 2019.

Yung Lean song, presumably produced by Gud based on his credit, that was sampled into the intro of "Dubai Shit". The pair weren't initially credited and Gud implied on Twitter that they weren't paid either; Gud put the blame on the labels rather than Travis or Quavo. 2/3 of the relevant tweets have since been deleted.
In an interview with DJBooth, producer FKi 1st said that "5% Tint" was started on January 4 or 5, 2018, in the same session that "RIP Screw" was created. It's unclear exactly what was done.
In an interview with The FADER, Mike Dean stated that "5% Tint" was basically finished before he added the outro, which was his first production contribution to the album.
Early version, titled "Thru My Window", that is missing a verse according to a scene from 'Look Mom I Can Fly'.

Snippet that leaked not long before the album dropped; said to have originated from a relative of someone who was given the song for sample clearance reasons, as they were involved in the creation of the Goodie Mob song that it samples. Most likely identical to the final release, but unlikely to be the final mix.

Sonny hesitated to call Travis's version of the song the original, further suggesting that this version may be quite different.

It was previously unclear, if Don was featured on this version, but a comment of his ("Good Times") on a post about the song seemingly confirmed his contribution.

My Eyes [Demo 1]

[prod. Justin Vernon]

This would date it at 2019-20, but Justin Vernon played a snippet of his demo on Instagram Live in May 2021, which would be a strange thing to do for a releasing song. This earliest version likely uses the more simplistic production found in Justin's demo.

The snippet that can be heard is likely a newer version though (see 'Demo 2'), since the clip was most likely filmed in 2023. Travis also seemingly teased some of the lyrics on a caption of a Instagram post shortly before Astrofest 2021.

Seemingly rough copy of 'Utopia' dated May 14, 2020, featuring Kanye West performing freestyles over some beats, likely meant as a reference; no Travis vocals. Ye was rumoured to be executive producing the album in 2020, and it appears that this is from that time. Features 15 tracks (with some having two versions each), including early versions of "Delresto" and "Big Champ". Besides the latter, no other title is known. A deleted tweet from Lil Bibby in 2021 confirmed that Travis was intended to appear on an early version of the posthumous Juice WRLD album 'The Party Never Ends'. Reliable sources have since confirmed that the songs Travis recorded for were "KTM Drip" and "Purple Substance", and that his verses ended up being scrapped due to Juice's manager Pete disliking them. Travis Scott was added after a fake tracklist, with him being added to "KTM Drip", went viral.

A deleted tweet from Lil Bibby in 2021 confirmed that Travis was intended to appear on an early version of the posthumous Juice WRLD album 'The Party Never Ends'. Reliable sources have since confirmed that the songs Travis recorded for were "KTM Drip" and "Purple Substance", and that his verses ended up being scrapped due to Juice's manager Pete disliking the verses. Another attempt to release the song occured, when it was picked as one of the non-fan-chosen songs for the deluxe of 'TPNE' in early 2025, but it never ended up dropping.

During CHASE B's Apple Music Radio appearance on January 15, 2026, him and Sheck Wes mentioned a song with Q-Tip that's "pretty much unheard" and was made during the same sessions that also originated "Out West" and "Mayday". Unconfirmed since we don't really know if Travis Scott is actually on the song.

Collaborative project with Kid Cudi that was effectively announced on April 23, 2020, when the title track was released. Other songs from the project are known about, but the album never materialised. Cudi has made multiple tweets over the years addressing the status of the project, but it is currently, as of August 2024, not planned for release (see Sources).

In an interview with Rolling Stone, Bad Bunny mentioned that him and Travis had "worked on ["K-pop"] a while back"; The Weeknd's lyrics suggest that he may have been added around May 2023 during the sessions in southern France, so there's likely an early version without him. However this might've been disproven by Rob49 in a interview with RapTV, where he said Travis played the song with "The Weeknd and Bad Bunny" for him and asked to hop on the track (which he passed. This was in July 2022). Rob has indeed stated in multiple occasions that Travis had played a song with Abel for him (citing Bad Bunny only that one time though). Supposedly, this was the first remix before Travis was replaced with Herbo, but ultimately Kodak was the one that was cut. Remains a rumour unless it surfaces.

In a 2025 GQ Cover, Pusha T recalled Travis playing 'Utopia' for Pharrell (while he was also seemingly present) in May 2023 in Paris. Pusha claimed that Travis hid Drake's part off of "Meltdown" (which is mostly a diss towards Pharrell) when playing the songs. However, it can be verified that Drake hadn't recorded anything for the song yet (he only recorded in July); this was also confirmed by Travis in a 2026 Rolling Stone interview. This possibly confirms a Travis solo demo; it's also worth considering though that the song might've never be played in that occasion, with Pusha either making it up (for the sake of the "beef" with Travis), or he could've just be misremembering; matter of fact, based on other loosy infos, the song possibly didn't even exist at that time and only started days before the album released (although that is still unconfirmed too). And even more, some Travis' lyrics on the song directly reference Drake's, so the situation is not very clear.

In a interview with RapTV, Rob49 said Travis played songs off of 'Utopia' for him (verifiably in July 2022). He revealed that Travis played "his stuff with Pharrell", possibly referring to a unheard collaboration. It's also a safe guess to say it could've just been either "Down In Atlanta" (although there are no Pharrell vocals) or"At the Party"; also, if any of these were the case, it doesn't necessarily mean they would've been intended for 'Utopia', Travis might've just played them for the sake of it.

Supposed early version of the project that leaked on KTT on June 1, 2013. Pharaohxx sold it to KTT user Fitz, who leaked it. The original, unsequenced order of the files is as follows: 

01. 12 Disciples
02. Manor Ruff Beat
03. Night
04. Gold 88
05. Travis x Chip
06. Dust
07. Time Machine
08. travis1
09. Illamerica
10. Paradise V3
11. Phoenix
12. Gifted Beat
13. 16 Chapels
14. Owl Pharaoh Intro

It's unclear whether this is a genuine early version of the project or simply a collection of random demos. Pharaoxx stated that he left out the original version of "Sin City" and the version of "Gold 88" with Pusha T, which suggests that it's merely a compilation, though the inclusion of songs like "16 Chapels", "Night", and "Illamerica", which were all already released/leaked, suggest that the leak was an early version of the project, or at the very least a collection of songs that were being considered for the project. 

KTT user mikelangelo (who leaked the LQ version of "Sin City" and knew Travis) claimed to have heard a very similar version of the project over a phonecall with Travis. mikelangelo stated that the call took place in December 2012, so it's possible that he heard a later version; if this is the case, the songs being unfinished here makes more sense, and Pharaohxx's comment about leaving things out may have been a bluff. We know that "Gold 88" had Pusha T from July 28, 2012, at the latest, so it's possible that this is a version of the project from before he was added.

There are three probable scenarios (in no particular order): 1) this is a random compilation of demos that Pharaohxx had on-hand; 2) this is a collection of songs that were being considered for the project (effectively an early version) sometime close to July 2012; or 3) this is an incomplete version of the project from December 2012 that is missing some songs and some correct versions of songs.

Untitled Album [V2] [July 2013 - Early 2014]
(Source: Travis Scott, Pharaohxx)
(AKA: Fuego)

During an interview on HOT97 on July 16, 2013, Travis officially announced that he was working on an album, set to be released in 2014. At the time of the announcement, Travis said that he had recently been working with Crada, so any songs made with him are likely in contention; "Naked" and "Can't Look In My Eyes" may have still been in consideration (assuming Cudi hadn't taken the latter back yet) but "Armageddon" likely wasn't. Pharaohxx also said that the Rick Rubin rework of "Naked" would make the album. An October 31, 2013, Rolling Stone article said that Travis was considering 'Fuego' as a title, but 'Rodeo' was eventually settled on (in March 2014 at the latest). The project continued to evolve until eventually all songs from this time had been cut.

By August 19, 2014, according to GOODMUSICINSIDER, 'Rodeo' already had features from James Blake, Justin Vernon of Bon Iver, and King Krule, as well as a song with PARTYNEXTDOOR and Young Thug that we now know is "Perfect" [V2]. Other probable songs include "Pray 4 Love" and "Love".

"Nightcrawler" is listed as "Night Call", which was its new title from June onwards; it was also titled this on the CD leak, though it was changed back to "Nightcrawler" for the official release. "Pray 4 Love" is listed as "Pray For Love". "My Ex Best Friend" has been crossed out, and a song has been erased. Possible contenders for the erased song are "Twist One Up" and "Poet and Parade" (which were both revisited and reconsidered for the album shortly before this tracklist was penned), and "Joyride" (which was sent to Rihanna around this time); the latter two are the only two songs that look like they could fit what is left of the erased text, but "Poet and Parade" is currently the most likely.

Based on information from a producer that has worked extensively with Cactus Jack, as well as what can be inferred from comments made by Travis as part of the GQ Cover 'The Reemergence of Travis Scott', Travis intended to release a mixtape titled 'Dystopia' in late 2021 or very early 2022, followed by the album 'Utopia' in early 2022. While the producer should be a reliable source, they also claimed that they had production on both projects, but didn't appear on 'Utopia'; it's still possible that they were telling the truth and were simply cut. Regardless, the aforementioned GQ Cover states that Travis intended "other releases" after the "Escape Plan" / "Mafia" pack - quite possibly 'Dystopia' - but the AstroFest tragedy halted the plans. 

While no tracks are confirmed, some can be speculated on. "Heartbeat" would be a solid contender. KayCyy mentioned that it was intended to be the lead single for 'Utopia' before the leaks messed it up; it could be that he was partially correct, and the song was intended for 'Dystopia' instead. Snippets like "I Need You*" would also fit the supposed darker vibe that 'Dystopia' was rumoured to have. It could also be the case that several of the darker songs on 'Utopia' were repurposed from 'Dystopia'.

According to Travis in the GQ Cover 'The Reemergence of Travis Scott', by the time that "Escape Plan" and "Mafia" were released on November 5, 2021, a version of 'Utopia' was roughly 50-60% completed. It can be deduced that a large number of the songs that made the final cut of the album existed at this time:

-"Hyaena", "Sirens", "Delresto" were all verifiably started in 2020
-"Thank God" and "God's Country" were started by Ye in 2020; it's not precisely known when Travis took them. Studio images shared by KayCyy after 'Utopia' released suggest that "Thank God" was reworked in June 2021
-"Lost Forever" was recorded with Gunn in February 2021
-The first part of "Skitzo" and "Til Further Notice" were verifiably started in 2019, altough the latter was a Metro song for sometime and it's not known precisely when Travis took it. The later parts of "Skitzo" could be from sometime from late 2020 to early 2021 based on Travis' lyrics, but this is pure speculation
-"Looove" was verifiably revisited in mid-2021
-The first part of "My Eyes" is said to be one of the first songs worked on the album according to Travis.
-"Future Sounds" was started in 2019, with Travis recording in 2020 and the last version being dated just before the release of 'Donda'. It's not known when Travis took the song, but SZA (and probably Future) definitely wasn't on the song until very late. It's a safer to guess it was reworked into "Telekinesis" by Travis after the tragedy
-"Modern Jam" is unclear; it has to be from 2021 at the earliest, but it may have been started after AstroFest

Due to the lack of clarity surrounding the 'Dystopia' / 'Utopia' situation, it's difficult to speculate on what other songs were in consideration at this time and whether or not all of the aforementioned songs were intended for the album. "In My Head" is the only solid guess, since it was briefly previewed at Travis's Dior x Cactus Jack show alongside "Escape Plan" and "Lost Forever". "Respected" and "Hold On" are said to be from 2020, possibly meant for the album, but totally unconfirmed as of now. "Franchise" was first advertised as the opener of the 'Utopia' era in 2020, but it's likely it was later cut from the project in 2021.
